ideas.title: The nightlight test
ideas.angle: Screen-test footage of kids describing what they'd say to Luma about being scared of the dark. Real voices, no script.
ideas.why_it_matters: Weekend-planning parents book with their hearts. Kids taking the theme seriously is the proof, not a claim.
ideas.hook: We asked kids what they'd tell a firefly who's afraid of the dark.
ideas.source: Audience Lab: 'she walked out asking for a firefly nightlight'

ideas.title: Lit by one light
ideas.angle: A story-room carousel showing how the harvest-night sequence was lit by a single source, frame by frame.
ideas.why_it_matters: Craft content earns the animation-devotee audience without a single superlative.
ideas.hook: Every frame of this sequence has exactly one light. Here's why.
ideas.source: Brand file: behind-the-scenes story room content is real

ideas.title: The imitation game
ideas.angle: A Reel of the octopus imitating each swim-team member, cut to end on the line 'except himself'. Comedy first, theme last.
ideas.why_it_matters: Teens share the gag; the finale line carries the meaning. Two audiences, one asset.
ideas.hook: He can do a perfect impression of everyone on the team.
ideas.source: Audience Lab: 'the swim meet finale where he stops imitating is perfect'

ideas.title: Repair stories
ideas.angle: Ask followers for the oldest thing they've ever fixed and kept. Feature the best with the sunflower-through-scrap key art.
ideas.why_it_matters: The maker audience saw themselves in the film; give them the microphone and they'll do the marketing.
ideas.hook: What's the oldest thing you've ever fixed instead of replaced?
ideas.source: Audience Lab: 'first film that gets why people love repairing things'

ideas.title: One thousand cranes, folded live
ideas.angle: A slow, wordless newsletter piece: an animator folds crane #1000 on camera at a kitchen table, lamplight only.
ideas.why_it_matters: The film's audience distrusts hype. Quiet confidence is the only register that sells it.
ideas.hook: It takes four minutes to fold a paper crane properly. We timed it.
ideas.source: Brand file: let quiet moments be quiet

ideas.title: Bring someone who taught you
ideas.angle: A ticket-bundle post built around bringing a grandparent, with real terms shown plainly in the asset.
ideas.why_it_matters: Multi-generational bookings are the film's whole box office; make the invitation explicit and the pricing honest.
ideas.hook: This winter, take the person who taught you something with their hands.
ideas.source: Audience Lab: 'call your grandmother after this one'
